LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS ARTURO PENA AND SUNSET SCAVENGER COMPANY'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE A CROSS-COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO JUNE 20, 2014 TO GIVE OPPOSING PARTY THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. OPPOSING PARTY MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN JUNE 10, 2014 AT 4:00 PM OR THE MOTION WILL BE GRANTED. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING AND REPLY PAPERS. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-18-2014 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-07-2014 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Dec-02-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jul-17-2014 Set For Case Management Conference On Oct-22-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Moiton And Motion For Leave To File A X-Complaint;
Off Calendar Jun-20-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. PETER C. CATALANOTTI OF MANNING & KASS, TEL. NO. 217-6990 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTs ARTURO PENA and SUNSET SCAVENGER COMPANY's (ERRONEOUSLY SUED HEREIN AS RECOLOGY SUNSET SCAVENGER COMPANY) Motion For Leave To File A Cross Complaint is Granted. (CCP sec. 428.10.) Defendants may file their proposed cross-complaint within 10 days of entry of this order. Defendants' objection to evidence is sustained as lacking authentication. (Evid. Code sec. 1400.) Defendants have established that the allegations in the cross-complaint arise out of the same transaction or occurrence as the complaint. It serves the interests of justice to have all issues relating to the same car accident resolved at once in order to avoid multiplicity of actions, promote judicial economy, and prevent the risk of inconsistent verdicts. Defendants have not shown prejudice by the filing of the cross-complaint. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/mjm

Notice Of Moiton And Motion For Leave To File A X-Complaint;
Off Calendar Jun-10-2014 Continued To Jun-20-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS ARTURO PENA AND SUNSET SCAVENGER COMPANY'S Motion For Leave To File A Cross-Complaint is continued to June 20, 2014 to give opposing party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Opposing party must deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than June 10, 2014 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be granted. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the moving and reply papers. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.
